text: So, the western peninsula of Russia just
got an unbelievable amount of snow, and
a ton of pictures and videos have been
going viral that show just how crazy it
is. There's even videos surfacing like
this one of people literally sliding
down their apartments. It doesn't even
look real. And well, that's because it
isn't. Yet, a ton of huge news outlets
keep on posting these as if they are
real. You see, Russia did get an ungodly
amount of snow. Here's a real picture of
a store that got buried. Here's a real
video of someone walking next to a
stoplight. And here's a real video of
someone jumping into the snow from their
building. The thing is, once this story
got enough traction, a bunch of AI slop
surfaced and got mixed into it to the
point where it's genuinely harder for me
to find real pictures and videos of the
snowstorm than it is for me to find fake
ones. It's actually concerning how much
misinformation we just witnessed. And I
can imagine it's only going to get
worse.
